title: AoIR 2025 Conference Agenda - Session Chair
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as the Chair for a paper proposal session titled 'Dynamics of Polarization: Unpacking Echo Chambers with Agent-Based Modeling' at the Association of Internet Researchers (AoIR) conference on November 20, 2025.
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as the Chair for a session titled 'Dynamics of Polarization: Unpacking Echo Chambers with Agent-Based Modeling' at the AoIR 2025 conference, which strongly aligns with his academic specialization.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This news article, published on January 9, 2026, discusses findings from a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, revealing that Facebook's algorithm gives excessive visibility to extremist and conspiracy content, impacting elections.
description: A news article from uniamo.news discusses findings from a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto, revealing how Facebook's algorithm promotes extremist and conspiracy content, impacting elections.

title: lumsanews.it
description: News article quoting Fabio Giglietto, Professor of Internet Studies at the University of Urbino, on the impact of Meta platforms, disinformation, and extremist content on elections, and the challenges of regulation.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: A news article from 'Open' on January 7, 2026, reports on a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithm penalizes official political content while boosting anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiracy theorist extremist content.
description: A news article from 'Open' reports on a study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithm amplifies extremist content. The study directly relates to his expertise in social media analysis and disinformation research.
